abstract: Get details about your channel, including the name, corresponding website, and default section.
overview:
## Discussion

Use the Read Channel Information endpoint to retrieve information about the channel, list of custom fonts used in the channel ([doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsAPI/Channel]), and link to the channel’s default section ([doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsAPI/ChannelLinks]). See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/making-an-http-request-to-the-apple-news-api] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/signing-the-http-request] in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/apple-news-api-tutorial].

You create a channel in News Publisher, not the Apple News API. See [https://support.apple.com/guide/news-publisher/apd1c0277d3e] in News Publisher User Guide.

### Example

# Read Channel Information

**Web Service Endpoint**

Get details about your channel, including the name, corresponding website, and default section.

## Discussion

Use the Read Channel Information endpoint to retrieve information about the channel, list of custom fonts used in the channel ([doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsAPI/Channel]), and link to the channel’s default section ([doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsAPI/ChannelLinks]). See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/making-an-http-request-to-the-apple-news-api] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/signing-the-http-request] in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/apple-news-api-tutorial].

You create a channel in News Publisher, not the Apple News API. See [https://support.apple.com/guide/news-publisher/apd1c0277d3e] in News Publisher User Guide.

### Example



## Channel

- **Read Channel Quota Information**: Get details about your channel’s remaining quota for sending create and update requests, the queue size, and wait time.
- **Channel**: See the fields the read channel endpoint returned.
- **ChannelLinks**: See the links the read channel endpoint returned.
- **ChannelResponse**: See which objects make up the channel response.
